programmes.show_description: Keloidcon. Alonso's lipomas are holding him back at the gym. A comic book enthusiast deals with lumps on her face. Jeffrey has a leaky head lump named Larry. S5, Ep14
Veiny Balloons. Robert carries the weight of the huge lump on his head. Christina has big, veiny, liquid-filled balloons next to her eyes. Felipe has severe dry skin. S5, Ep15
Tara's Story. Tara is single mother of two who feels like a prisoner in her own body. Riddled with joint pain she is determined to lose the weight - and see her kids grow up. S2, Ep8
Amber's Story. At age 23, and over 600 lbs, Amber relies on her family for even simple needs. Her fear of dying young sends her 2,000 miles for weight loss surgery. S3, Ep1
The Struggle Is Real. If Chef Huda can't get her entourage to agree, this contest between ball gown and mermaid will have no winners, just losers! Plus, Valerie seeks a black dress. S19, Ep4
I'm Not Crying, You're Crying. Worlds collide as Alex looks for a black gown fit for Morticia Adams and Lucille Ball. Prashanthi's future brother-in-law asks to see a topless dress! S19, Ep5
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Mudlick Or Bust. Molly surprises Peggy by reuniting her with her estranged sister, Rosemary, when they take a road trip to Peggy's hometown of Mudlick. S5, Ep17
No Kay Morale. When Peggy's depressed friend Kay returns to town, Molly is determined to find a way to lift her spirits. S5, Ep18
Mother From Another Mudlick. Mike meets his Aunt Rosemary for the first time and is instantly smitten by her warmth and positive attitude, which is the opposite of his mother. S5, Ep19
Poker, Faith And Eggs. When George Sr. is rushed to the emergency room, Meemaw comes to babysit. And the kids have an adventure getting to the hospital on their own. S1, Ep3
A Therapist, A Comic Book. Sheldon refuses to eat solid food after a choking scare but finds solace in a new interest after he discovers comic books. S1, Ep4
A Solar Calculator, A Game Ball. Sheldon uses his statistics skills to help his father coach the football team. Both Sheldon and Georgie struggle with the popularity this brings. S1, Ep5
The Nerdvana Annihilation. When the delivery of a full-size prop of 1960's 'The Time Machine' causes Penny to miss work, she scolds Leonard about his sci-fi collectibles. S1, Ep14
The Pork Chop Indeterminacy. The gang fall for Sheldon's twin sister, and Leonard's plan to win her heart is derailed when Sheldon declares he'll decide who can date her. S1, Ep15
The Peanut Reaction. Penny wants to throw a surprise party for Leonard, but Sheldon's indulgence at an electronics store throws her off-track. S1, Ep16
The Tangerine Factor. Penny's breakup spurs Leonard to summon the courage to ask her out, and they both go to Sheldon for advice on their upcoming date. S1, Ep17
The Bad Fish Paradigm. Penny opens up to Sheldon after a less-than-stellar first date with Leonard, placing him in the uncomfortable position of having to keep a secret. S2, Ep1
The Codpiece Topology. Seeing Penny with another guy sends Leonard back to Leslie Winkle, much to Sheldon's dismay. S2, Ep2
The Barbarian Sublimation. Penny becomes addicted to an online game based on the world of Conan the Barbarian and persistently pesters Sheldon for advice. S2, Ep3
The Griffin Equivalency. Full of confidence following being named one of People's 30 to Watch Under 30, Raj hits on Penny and becomes impossibly arrogant. S2, Ep4
Fight To The Finish. When Molly gets angry with Mike for not listening to her vent about writing with Peggy, they go their separate ways for a day. S5, Ep20
Near Death Do Us Part. After Carl has a near-death experience while on duty, he tells Mike he's going to propose to Victoria. But can Mike keep a secret? S5, Ep21
The Bitter Man And The Sea. Mike and Molly are forced to 'uninvite' Carl to their anniversary cruise following his nasty breakup with Victoria. S5, Ep22
A Patch, A Modem And A Zantac. After being dismissed in class, Sheldon is determined to prove a visiting NASA representative wrong and starts studying theoretical physics. S1, Ep6
A Brisket, Voodoo And Cannonball Run. Meemaw refuses to give George Sr. her brisket recipe. The kids try to think of ways to resolve this family feud. S1, Ep7
Cape Canaveral. George takes his sons to see a space shuttle launch. Meanwhile, Mary, Meemaw and Missy have a girl's night at the salon. S1, Ep8
The Euclid Alternative. An intervention is used to try to force Sheldon to overcome his fear of driving. S2, Ep5
The Cooper-Nowitzki Theorem. Sheldon and his friends are mystified when a younger undergraduate student is attracted to him. S2, Ep6
The Panty Pinata Polarization. When Sheldon bans Penny from the apartment, she decides to retaliate. Howard and Raj search for the America's Next Top Model house. S2, Ep7
The Lizard-Spock Expansion. Sheldon wants to use an alternate version of rock-paper-scissors to help resolve conflicts. A girl Wolowitz has a major crush on falls for Leonard. S2, Ep8
The White Asparagus Triangulation. Sheldon is over-exuberant that Leonard finally has a girlfriend matching his high standards. S2, Ep9
The Vartabedian Conundrum. Leonard seeks Penny's counsel when his relationship with Stephanie takes. Sheldon's hypochondriac tendencies get the better of him. S2, Ep10
The Bath Item Gift Hypothesis. Sheldon irks the gangs with his obsession with proper Christmas gift protocol. Leonard's crushed when Penny dates a colleague of his. S2, Ep11
The Killer Robot Instability. Penny's criticism of Wolowitz's romantic life pushes him into depression and AWOL for a fighting robot battle the guys are depending on him for. S2, Ep12
To Get A Diagnosis Is Everything. Ryan has excruciating sores in his groin and a cyst on his neck. Dr Emma delivers a diagnosis, but he must give up smoking to finally recover. S8, Ep8
It's My Third Boob. Bianca has one of the most challenging keloids that Dr Emma has ever seen. Ben must overcome his fear of needles to have his 12-year-old cyst removed. S6, Ep6
A Prisoner In My Own Body. Amy wears a mask of makeup to hide the angry red blotches on her face. Dr Emma races to remove a tricky lipoma before the anaesthetic wears off. S6, Ep7
Scaly Mermaid Skin. Natasha's massive ear keloids are keeping her from pursuing her modelling dreams. Eight-year-old Wynter wants her scaly mermaid skin cured. S5, Ep16
Tales From The Cyst. Nhan has an extremely red birthmark that covers half of his face. Debbie worries that her shoulder lump will prematurely age her. S5, Ep17
Susan's Story. At over 600 lbs, Susan is committed to drastic weight loss. But when a medical disorder strikes her mobility, her plight becomes a matter of life and death. S3, Ep2
Pauline's Story. At nearly 700 lbs Pauline has an addiction to food that is killing her and taken over the life of her son. Can she overcome it and save both their lives? S3, Ep3
Our Dress Has To Go Viral. Amanda has been obsessing over a Randy Fenoli dress, but will it live up to the hype? Kindra and her pastor mother can't agree on a suitable dress. S19, Ep6
Ugly Duckling. After transitioning, Chloe hopes to find a dress that makes her feel like her true self. Country singer Jenny needs a dress that suits her snowy wedding vision. S19, Ep7
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.

Cops On The Rocks. Mike and Carl decide to go to couples counselling to try and reconcile their partnership. Plus, Molly decides to do a promotional radio interview. S6, Ep1
One Small Step For Mike. Molly has high hopes that a fitness tracking device will encourage Mike to be more active. But Mike has other plans for it. S6, Ep2
Peg O' My Heart Attack. After Peg suffers a heart attack after a heated argument with Molly, Mike blames his wife, who immediately sets out to make amends. S6, Ep3
Spock, Kirk And Testicular Hernia. George Sr. makes a deal with Sheldon to tutor his brother when Georgie's poor grades threaten his place on the football team. S1, Ep9
Eagle Feather, String Bean. Sheldon interviews with a school for gifted children. Much to their surprise, the rest of the family struggle with his absence. S1, Ep10
Demons, Sunday School. A worried Mary sends Sheldon to Sunday school after she finds him playing Dungeons & Dragons with Tam and Billy. S1, Ep11
The Friendship Algorithim. Looking to get friendly with Barry Kripke to gain access to a piece of high-tech lab equipment, Sheldon develops a flowchart process to make friends. S2, Ep13
The Financial Permeability. When Sheldon loans money to Penny so she can pay her rent, it leads to Leonard coming face-to-face with her ex, Kurt. S2, Ep14
The Maternal Capacitance. A visit from Leonard's mother ends up bringing him and Penny closer. S2, Ep15
The Cushion Saturation. Paintball leads to a squabble between Sheldon and Penny as well as romance between Howard and Leslie. S2, Ep16
The Terminator Decoupling. The gang is thrilled to see Summer Glau is also aboard their train to San Francisco. Sheldon frets over having forgotten a paper he wants to present. S2, Ep17
The Work Song Nanocluster. Penny's business selling hair accessories out of her apartment gets out of hand when an over-caffeinated Sheldon takes charge. S2, Ep18
The Dead Hooker Juxtaposition. Penny feels threatened when another sexy woman moves into the apartment building and angered when she senses the guys are being taken advantage of. S2, Ep19
The Hofstadter Isotope. A fellow comic book enthusiast's hitting on Penny leads to Leonard, Raj and Howard going to a bar's Ladies' Night. S2, Ep20
The Act Of Tinseling. Pedro tells Liz he worries Sophie isn't ready for a relationship. Cortney and Colt still have intimacy issues, as she wonders about his intentions. S5, Ep2
Why I Want To Be A Monogamist. Christine and David visit Janelle. Kody and Robyn discuss their now monogamist relationship and the possibility of bringing in a new wife. S13, Ep1
